segments.paradiso-21-g21: Qual savesse opens an unreal/hypothetical clause: Qual functions impersonally as 'if anyone' or 'whoever', and savesse is the imperfect subjunctive used for a contrary-to-fact or potential situation. qual era la pastura del viso mio ne l’aspetto beato is a descriptive subordinate: the second qual = 'what', era is the imperfect indicative describing the ongoing state ('was'), pastura literally 'sustenance' used figuratively for what nourished/animated his face, ne = 'in' (archaic contraction of in + lo), and l’aspetto beato = 'that/the blessed aspect'. quand’ io mi trasmutai ad altra cura is a temporal clause: quand’ (elision of quando) = 'when', mi is the reflexive pronoun with trasmutai (passato remoto, 1st person) meaning 'I changed/turned myself', and ad altra cura = 'to/onto another care/concern' (ad = a).
segments.paradiso-21-g22: conoscerebbe is the 3rd-person conditional ('would know'), completing the hypothetical introduced earlier. quanto m’era a grato is an idiomatic, inverted phrase meaning 'how pleasing it was to me': quanto = 'how'/'how much', m' = mi = indirect object 'to me', era = imperfect 'was', a grato = predicate 'pleasing to'. ubidire a la mia celeste scorta is an infinitive clause complementing quanto: ubidire (archaic form of 'obbedire') = 'to obey', a = 'to', la mia celeste scorta = 'my heavenly escort/guide' (scorta = escort or guide).
segments.paradiso-21-g23: contrapesando is a present participle/gerund ('weighing' or 'counterbalancing') that indicates the manner in which the previous sentiment is measured. l’un con l’altro lato is an idiomatic, slightly elliptical expression: l’un = 'one', con = 'with', l’altro lato = 'the other side' — together 'weighing one side against the other'. Note the archaic elisions (l’ for lo) and compact word order typical of Dante.
segments.paradiso-21-g24: Dentro al cristallo is a locative phrase: 'dentro' (inside) + the contracted article 'al' (a + il = to/the) giving 'inside the crystal'. 'che 'l vocabol porta' is a relative clause introduced by 'che' modifying 'cristallo'; here 'porta' is used as a verb meaning 'bears/carries' (archaic usage), so the clause means 'that the word bears' or more naturally 'that bears the word'. Note the elision ’l for il after che (che ’l).
segments.paradiso-21-g25: 'cerchiando il mondo' is a present participle phrase (cerchiando = encircling) that specifies what the crystal (or the word on it) is doing: 'encircling the world'. The participle phrase sits parenthetically and links forward to the following noun phrase.
segments.paradiso-21-g26: 'del suo caro duce' is a genitive/partitive phrase ('of its/his dear leader') that attaches to the preceding idea (the world encircled). 'sotto cui giacque ogne malizia morta' is a relative clause: 'sotto' = under, 'cui' = whom/which (referring to the leader), 'giacque' is the perfect/preterite of giacere ('lay'), and 'ogne malizia morta' is 'every malice dead' (morta as adjective). Together: 'of its dear leader, under whom every malice lay dead.'
segments.paradiso-21-g27: 'di color d’oro' is a descriptive prepositional phrase 'of a golden color' (d’ = di). 'in che raggio traluce' is a relative clause modifying that color: 'in which a ray shines through' (traluce < traluccere). 'vid’ io' is an archaic inversion of the perfective verb with the subject pronoun after it (vid' = vidi, 'I saw'); the elision mark after vid' reflects the dropped final vowel. 'uno scaleo eretto in suso tanto' places the noun 'uno scaleo' (one stair/flight) with participle 'eretto' (erected/raised) and spatial adverb 'in suso' (upward); 'tanto' begins a correlative result clause 'so (much)... that...'.
segments.paradiso-21-g28: 'che nol seguiva la mia luce' is a result clause introduced by 'che' after 'tanto': 'that it did not/nor did my light follow it.' 'nol' is the archaic contraction of non + lo = 'not it' (i.e., 'it did not'). 'seguiva' is imperfect tense of seguire ('follow'), used here to describe a continued inability of the speaker's light to follow the stair; 'la mia luce' is the subject/agent 'my light'.
segments.paradiso-21-g29: Vidi anche 'I also saw' (vidi = simple past). 'per li gradi' = 'along/through the steps' ('per' + plural article 'li'); 'scender giuso' is an infinitive phrase 'to descend down' used here after the perception verb to describe what was seen; 'tanti splendor' = 'so many/so great splendors' (tanti qualifies splendor). Word order places the adverbial path ('per li gradi') before the verb phrase for poetic effect.
segments.paradiso-21-g30: This is a result clause introduced by 'ch’' (che) after the visual perception: 'that I thought...' ('io pensai'). 'ch’ogne lume che par nel ciel' continues with an embedded relative clause: 'ogne lume' = 'every light', and 'che par nel ciel' = 'that appears in heaven' (par = appears, nel = in the + article). The sequence has two subordinations: 'I thought that [every light that appears in heaven ...]'.
segments.paradiso-21-g31: 'quindi fosse diffuso' completes the subordinate thought. 'quindi' here means 'from there' (indicating origin). 'fosse' is the imperfect subjunctive of essere, used after a verb of thought ('pensai che ... fosse') to report the content of the thought, and 'diffuso' is a past participle used adjectivally/passively meaning 'spread/diffused.' Together: 'was diffused from there.'
segments.paradiso-21-g32: "E come," is the connective opening a simile: "E" = "And" linking to what precedes, and "come" = "as" introducing the comparison. The comma marks the start of a comparative clause.
segments.paradiso-21-g33: "per lo natural costume" is a prepositional phrase meaning "by (or through) the natural/customary practice." "per" here has an instrumental/causal force ("by"), "lo" is the definite article, and "natural costume" = "natural custom/habit."
segments.paradiso-21-g34: "le pole insieme" is the subject phrase: "le" = the plural article; "pole" is the noun used here (contextually likely a group of birds or units) and is plural; "insieme" = "together." Word order (article + noun + adverb) is normal for a poetic line.
segments.paradiso-21-g35: "al cominciar del giorno" = "at the beginning of the day." "al" = a + il (to/at the), "cominciar" is an infinitive used as a noun (common in Italian poetry), and "del giorno" specifies whose beginning (of the day).
segments.paradiso-21-g36: "si movono a scaldar le fredde piume" is a clause of motion and purpose: "si movono" is reflexive plural present indicative (they move themselves / they set themselves in motion), "a scaldar" = "to warm" with the infinitive after the preposition "a" expressing purpose, and "le fredde piume" is the direct object (the cold feathers).
segments.paradiso-21-g37: "poi altre vanno via sanza ritorno" describes subsequent movements: "poi" = then/afterwards, "altre" = some others (feminine plural), "vanno via" = go away ("vanno" = they go; "via" here = away), "sanza" = archaic form of "senza" = without, and "ritorno" = return. The sequence lists different behaviors.
segments.paradiso-21-g38: "altre rivolgon sé onde son mosse" is another contrasted action: "altre" = other ones, "rivolgon" (rivolgono) = turn (here "turn themselves"), "sé" = themselves (reflexive), and the relative clause "onde son mosse" = "from which they were moved/started" ("onde" = whence/from which; "son mosse" = they have been moved/they were set in motion – "son" = are/have, "mosse" = past participle).
segments.paradiso-21-g39: "e altre roteando fan soggiorno" completes the catalogue: "e" = and, "altre" = others, "roteando" is a present participle (wheeling/rotating), "fan" = fanno = make/do, and "soggiorno" = a stay/pause. The participle precedes the finite verb poetically but modifies the subject.
segments.paradiso-21-g40: "tal modo parve me che quivi fosse in quello sfavillar che ’nsieme venne," is the report clause: "tal modo" = in such a way, "parve me" = it appeared to me (parve = passato remoto/poetic past of parere), and the subordinate clause introduced by "che" uses the imperfect subjunctive "fosse" after a verb of perception (parve) to express what seemed to exist there. "in quello sfavillar" uses an infinitive as a verbal noun ("the flashing/glittering"), and "che ’nsieme venne" = "which came together."
segments.paradiso-21-g41: "sì come in certo grado si percosse." closes the simile: "sì come" = just as, "in certo grado" = in a certain degree/step ("grado" can mean rank, step, or degree), and "si percosse" is the passato remoto (or poetic past) of "percuotere/percotere" meaning "it struck" or "it was struck"; the "si" gives a reflexive/impersonal flavor ("it struck itself/it occurred"). The clause compares how the flashing behaved to that striking at a certain degree.
segments.paradiso-21-g140: Dintorno a questa = 'around this one' (dintorno + a = a prepositional phrase). Vennero is the passato remoto, 3rd person plural of venire, 'they came'. The sequence 'vennero e fermarsi' is poetic: fermarsi appears as an infinitive with the reflexive particle attached, functioning here like the expected finite past form 'si fermarono' — i.e. 'and they stopped/hal ted'.
segments.paradiso-21-g141: e = 'and', linking to the previous clause. Fero here is an adjective (poetic/archaic form) meaning 'fierce/harsh' and modifies 'un grido' (the adjective appears before the noun for emphasis). 'Un grido' = 'a cry'; 'di sì alto suono' is a genitive/quality phrase introduced by di + the intensifier sì ('so') and alto suono = 'high/lofty sound' (here = loud, intense tone).
segments.paradiso-21-g142: Che introduces a result/consequence clause: 'that...'. Potrebbe is the present conditional of potere, 'could' or 'would be able to'. Assomigliarsi is the reflexive infinitive 'to be like/be compared (to)', used here impersonally/passively: 'could not here be likened'. Qui is the adverb 'here' modifying the ability to compare.
segments.paradiso-21-g143: Né = 'nor', continuing the negation from the previous clause. Word order places the subject io after the negative particle. Lo is the object pronoun 'it' (written like the article lemma given) placed before the verb. ’Ntesi is a contracted form of intesi/ntesi (passato remoto of intendere/intendere in older spelling) meaning 'I heard' or 'I understood'; here it means 'I heard', so the clause = 'nor did I hear it.'
segments.paradiso-21-g144: Sì is an adverb meaning 'so' or 'so much' (not 'yes') and intensifies the following verb. Mi is the clitic object 'me'. Vinse is passato remoto, 3rd person singular of vincere, 'overcame' (active voice: 'it overcame me'). Il tuono = 'the thunder' (the loud peal/sound that overwhelmed Dante).
segments.paradiso-21-g1: Già (adverb) = 'already.' Eran is the archaic imperfect 3rd-person-plural of essere (modern erano) and here combines with the past participle rifissi to state a past, continuing state: 'were fixed (again).' Rifissi is a past participle used predicatively; al = a + il 'to the' and de is the archaic form of di 'of,' so al volto de la mia donna = 'upon the face of my lady.'
segments.paradiso-21-g2: e introduces a coordinate clause 'and.' L’animo (with the elision l’ = the) is here the mind/spirit that goes with the eyes; con essi uses con + the pronoun essi ('with them') to link the spirit to the eyes.
segments.paradiso-21-g3: e da ogne altro intento s’era tolto is a perfective statement: s’ (reflexive) + era (imperfect/auxiliary) + tolto (past participle) = 'had withdrawn itself / had been removed' from every other intento (purpose/attention). Ogne is the archaic form of ogni 'every.'
segments.paradiso-21-g4: E = 'and.' Quella 'that one' or 'she' refers back to the lady; non + ridea is an archaic imperfect of ridere (ridea = was laughing / laughed), here negative: 'she did not laugh.'
segments.paradiso-21-g5: ma = 'but.' The line opens direct speech with S’io ridessi, where S’ is an older contraction of se 'if' + io 'I' and ridessi is the imperfect subjunctive ('if I were to laugh'); this subordinate clause introduces a hypothetical that follows in the main clause.
segments.paradiso-21-g6: mi cominciò = 'she began to me' or 'she began (speaking) to me'; cominciò is the 3rd-person-singular passato remoto/past 'began' and mi is the indirect object 'to me.'
segments.paradiso-21-g7: tu ti faresti is a conditional: faresti (2nd-person conditional of fare) with reflexive ti = 'you would make yourself / you would become.' Quale fu Semelè quando di cener fessi is a comparative example 'such as Semele was when (she) was reduced to ashes' — quando + de(c) + cener (ashes) describes the time/condition. Note archaic forms and compressed syntax in the mythological allusion.
segments.paradiso-21-g8: ché here = 'for' or 'because,' introducing an explanation. La bellezza mia = 'my beauty,' with the possessive after the noun (typical and idiomatic in poetry).
segments.paradiso-21-g9: che introduces a relative clause modifying bellezza; per le scale de l’etterno palazzo 'by/through the stairways of the eternal palace' uses per = 'through/along' and de = archaic di. Più s’accende uses the reflexive s’ + accende (present) to mean 'becomes more kindled/bright' as one ascends.
segments.paradiso-21-g10: com’ hai veduto = contraction of come hai veduto 'as you have seen'; com’ is an elision of come and hai veduto is present perfect 'you have seen,' referring back to the reader/listener's prior view.
segments.paradiso-21-g11: quanto più si sale = 'the more one climbs/ascends' (quanto più ... si sale is a comparative construction: the more X, the more Y). Si is the impersonal/reciprocal/reflexive pronoun used to form a general 'one/you' subject.
segments.paradiso-21-g12: se non si temperasse is a counterfactual subordinate clause: se non + si + temperasse (imperfect subjunctive) = 'if it were not tempered / restrained.' Temperasse is subjunctive because it conditions the result expressed next.
segments.paradiso-21-g13: tanto splende = 'it would shine so much' (tanto intensifies the verb); splende is present or potential 'shines,' here understood as the hypothetical result of the previous 'if' clause (classical poetry often compresses tense/mood relations).
segments.paradiso-21-g14: che introduces the result clause: 'that your mortal podere' — l' (the) tuo mortal podere = 'your mortal power/ability.' Mortale/podere is a compound notion: your human strength or power.
segments.paradiso-21-g15: al suo fulgore = 'before/at its radiance/brightness' (a + il = al); suo refers back to the lady's beauty, and fulgore is the noun 'brightness' or 'splendour' functioning as the reference point for the comparison.
segments.paradiso-21-g16: sarebbe is the conditional of essere 'would be.' Fronda (leafy bough) is the predicate nominative; che introduces a relative clause giving the metaphor: trono scoscende 'that brings a throne down' (scoscende = causes to descend/come off). The whole simile: your mortal power would be as a bough that topples a throne.
segments.paradiso-21-g17: Noi sem levati is an archaic/periphrastic perfect: sem (old form for siamo) + levati (past participle) = 'we have risen' or 'we rose.' Al settimo splendore = 'to the seventh splendour,' where al = a + il.
segments.paradiso-21-g18: che introduces a relative clause describing that splendour. Sotto 'l petto del Leone ardente = 'beneath the breast of the burning/ardent Lion' (sotto = under); raggia mo misto giù del suo valore is compact: mo = now, misto = 'mixed' (postpositive), giù = down, and del suo valore = 'from/of its power/valor.' The verb or noun raggia functions to indicate a ray/emanation now mixed and sent downward (poetic compression and older spellings are at work).
segments.paradiso-21-g19: Ficca is the 2nd-person singular imperative of ficcare 'stick/put/insert' (addressing 'tu' implicitly). Di retro a = 'behind' (literally 'of back to'), li occhi tuoi = 'your eyes' (archaic word order), and la mente is the direct object 'the mind' — so 'place/set your mind behind your eyes.'
segments.paradiso-21-g20: e fa is a second imperative joining the previous command (fa here functions as an imperative 'make/do'); di quelli specchi = 'of those mirrors' (quelli = those). a la figura = 'to the figure/likeness' and che ’n questo specchio ti sarà parvente is a relative clause: in this mirror (’n = in) it will seem/appear to you (sarà parvente = will be apparent/seeming to you).
segments.paradiso-21-g62: Tu is the emphatic subject pronoun 'you'. hai is the present indicative of avere ('you have') and governs the direct object l’udir (elided from il udir), a noun meaning 'hearing' or 'the sense of hearing'. mortal is an adjective modifying udir ('mortal' = human/limited). sì come is a comparative phrase 'just as' linking the two possessions; il viso ('the face' / here 'sight') is the parallel object that completes the comparison — the verb 'have' applies to both 'hearing' and 'sight'.
segments.paradiso-21-g63: rispuose is the archaic/poetic past tense (rispose) of rispondere: 'he answered'. a me is the indirect object 'to me'; the subject is omitted (implicit 'he' — the speaker who is answering). The semicolon marks the end of the reporting clause and leads into the following explanation.
segments.paradiso-21-g64: onde functions as 'therefore'/'hence' introducing a result or reason. qui = 'here' (in this place/matter). non si canta uses the impersonal/reflective si to mean 'one does not sing' or 'it is not sung' (no human singing takes place). per quel che literally 'for that which' = 'because' / 'for the fact that', introducing the reason clause; Bëatrice non ha riso is a present perfect ('has not smiled' / 'has not laughed') giving the factual cause for the absence of song.
segments.paradiso-21-g65: Giù per li gradi de la scala santa: Giù 'down'; per here means 'through' or 'along' (motion through the steps); li gradi 'the steps' (gradi = steps/stages) of la scala santa 'the holy ladder'. discesi is the first-person past of discendere ('I descended'). tanto sol = tanto + sol (sol = solo = 'only' / 'merely'), so 'so much only' = 'only so far' or 'merely' (emphatic limit). per farti festa uses per + infinitive to indicate purpose 'in order to' and farti is fare + clitic ti ('to make/do for you', i.e., to celebrate/honor you) — here the provided senses list for farti doesn't fit the verb+clitic sense, so that particular listed sense is not used. col dire e con la luce 'with speech/words and with the light' links means (speech and light) used to honor; che mi ammanta is a relative clause 'which mantles/veils me' where ammanta (ammantare) is present indicative 'mantles'.
segments.paradiso-21-g66: né più amor mi fece esser più presta: né più = 'nor more' (negative continuative). amor mi fece esser = 'love made me be' (fece is the past of fare used causatively: 'made' or 'caused' + infinitive esser 'to be'). più presta = 'more ready' (presta here is an adjective 'ready' — note the supplied senses for presta don't match the adjectival meaning, so that supplied sense isn't used). The clause states that no greater love caused him to be more ready.
segments.paradiso-21-g67: ché più e tanto amor quinci sù ferve: ché = 'for'/'because' introducing the reason. più e tanto amor = 'more and so much love' (intensifying). quinci sù = 'from here upward' (quinci = from here; sù = up). ferve is present tense of fervere 'to boil, seethe' used figuratively 'seethes' — the image is of love rising here from the court.
segments.paradiso-21-g68: sì come il fiammeggiar ti manifesta: sì come = 'just as'/'as' introducing a simile. il fiammeggiar is a verbal noun 'the flaming' or 'the flashing of flame' (the spectacle of flame). ti manifesta = 'shows you' or 'manifests to you' (manifesta is present indicative). Together: 'as the flaming shows you.'
segments.paradiso-21-g69: Ma = 'but'. l’alta carità = 'the high/lofty charity' (l' alta; carità here is the theological virtue 'charity' / divine love). This is the subject phrase that the following relative clause will qualify.
segments.paradiso-21-g70: che ci fa serve pronte al consiglio che ’l mondo governa: che introduces a relative clause 'which'. ci = 'us' (indirect object). fa is causative 'makes' and governs the infinitive serve 'to serve' — together 'makes us serve' or 'makes us ready to serve'. pronte is an adjective 'ready' agreeing with an implied plural object. al consiglio = 'to the counsel' (purpose/role), che ’l mondo governa = 'which governs the world' (a further relative clause modifying consiglio).
segments.paradiso-21-g71: sorteggia qui sì come tu osserve: sorteggia (from sorteggiare) literally 'draws lots' or 'allocates by lot' and here means 'assigns/appoints' (the subject 'charity' performs this action). qui = 'here'. sì come = 'just as' / 'in the way that'. tu osserve = 'you observe' (osserve, present tense, second person singular) — the speaker points out that this distribution occurs, as the listener can see.
segments.paradiso-21-g72: «Io veggio ben»,: Io 'I' is the subject pronoun. veggio here is used to mean 'I see'/'I perceive' in poetic diction (the provided senses list gives 'keep vigil/watch over', which does not match this usage), and ben = 'well' (adverb) — the speaker declares he perceives clearly.
segments.paradiso-21-g73: diss’ io,: diss' is an archaic contracted past form of dire (disse → diss'), meaning 'I said'. io is the emphatic subject repeated after the verb, a common stylistic device in poetry.
segments.paradiso-21-g74: «sacra lucerna,: sacra 'sacred' modifies lucerna 'lamp' or 'light' (vocative here: addressing the divine light as 'sacred lamp'). The comma sets up the direct address before the speaker continues.
segments.paradiso-21-g75: come libero amore in questa corte basta a seguir la provedenza etterna;: come = 'how' / 'as' introducing the explanatory clause. libero amore = 'free love' (libero modifying amore). in questa corte = 'in this court'. basta a seguir = basta is an impersonal/3rd-person verb 'is enough' or 'suffices', followed by a prepositional infinitive construction a + seguir 'to follow' (here 'suffices to follow'). la provedenza etterna = 'eternal providence' (direct object of seguir). The clause says that free love in the court is enough to follow eternal providence.
segments.paradiso-21-g76: ma questo è quel ch’a cerner mi par forte,: ma 'but' introduces a contrast. questo è quel ch' = 'this is the thing which' (identifying the difficult point). a cerner is an infinitive 'to discern' (cerner = discern), joined to mi par 'seems to me' (mi = to me; par = seems). forte here means 'difficult'/'strong' in sense 'hard to handle' — the whole line: 'but this is what seems hard for me to discern.'
segments.paradiso-21-g77: perché predestinata fosti sola a questo officio tra le tue consorte».: perché = 'because' introducing the reason/explanation. predestinata is a past participle used adjectivally 'predestined'. fosti is the archaic/past 2nd-person singular of essere 'you were' (here used addressing Beatrice: 'you were predestined'). sola = 'alone/only', a questo officio = 'to this office/duty', tra le tue consorte = 'among your companions' (consorte here used for 'companions'). Note: the provided senses for fosti in the word list did not match the verbal 'you were', so that listed sense is not used.
segments.paradiso-21-g131: Or = now. Voglion is the archaic/poetic form of vogliono (3rd-person plural present indicative) meaning “they want/ask.” The sequence quinci e quindi is an adverbial pair (“from here and from there”); chi + verb here introduces an indefinite subordinate clause (‘someone who…’) and the verbs rincalzi and meni appear in the present subjunctive form (3rd-person singular) after the volitive voglion — i.e. they want there to be someone who will ‘shore up’ (rincalzi) and someone who will ‘lead’ (meni) the pastors. The object li (= them) refers to “the modern pastors.”
segments.paradiso-21-g132: Tanto son gravi = 'they are so heavy/burdensome.' Son is the contracted poetic form of sono (3rd-person plural present indicative); tanto is an adverb meaning “so” or “so much” that intensifies gravi (plural adjective ‘burdensome/heavy’).
segments.paradiso-21-g133: E chi di rietro li alzi continues the pattern of indefinite clauses: e = and; chi = ‘whoever/someone’; di rietro = a prepositional phrase meaning ‘from behind’ (di + rietro); li = them; alzi is the present subjunctive (3rd-person singular) of alzare used in the same volitive construction begun by voglion, meaning ‘raise/lift up.’
segments.paradiso-21-g134: Cuopron is a dialectal/poetic spelling related to coprono (3rd-person plural present indicative) ‘they cover’; Dante places the verb before its complements (verb–object–subject order). D’i manti = di + i manti = ‘of the cloaks’ (here: with their cloaks), loro = their, and i palafreni = the palfreys (the mounts) is the noun phrase associated with the verb (either as subject or as the thing being covered, the exact syntactic angle is poetic/inverted).
segments.paradiso-21-g135: Sì che introduces a result clause: ‘so that’/‘so as to bring about.’ Due bestie van = ‘two beasts go’ with van = vanno (3rd-person plural present). Sott’ una pelle = sotto + elision = ‘under one skin,’ an idiomatic image; note the apostrophe marks Dante’s elision (sott’ una).
segments.paradiso-21-g136: An exclamatory apostrophe: oh pazïenza = ‘O patience’; che tanto sostieni = relative clause ‘that/which you sustain/endure so much.’ Sostieni is 2nd-person singular present indicative here and means ‘endure’ (you endure/put up with).
segments.paradiso-21-g137: This segment is a punctuation mark (a period) marking the end of the sentence. It contains no lexical items or grammatical structure to analyze and serves only to close the sentence.
segments.paradiso-21-g138: "A questa voce" = 'at (or on) that voice/sound' — the preposition a here introduces what is heard; "vid' io" is an elided past tense (vidi) with the pronoun io following the verb (poetic inversion) meaning 'I saw'. "più fiammelle" = 'more/larger number of little flames' (più = more). "di grado in grado" is a repetitive prepositional phrase meaning 'from degree to degree' or 'rank upon rank' (di ... in ... di ... in ... pattern), and the two infinitives "scendere e girarsi" are objects of vid' (verbs of perception can take bare infinitives): 'descend and turn themselves' (girarsi is reflexive, 'turn around').
segments.paradiso-21-g139: "e ogne giro" = 'and at every turn/circuit' (ogne = every; giro = turn/circuit). "le" is the feminine plural object pronoun ('them', referring back to the fiammelle) placed before the verb as is normal in Italian. "facea" is the archaic/imperfect form of fare (modern faceva) meaning 'made' or 'rendered' and expresses a repeated or ongoing action. "più belle" is the comparative 'more beautiful' (più + adjective).
segments.paradiso-21-g101: Definite article + noun: 'La mente' is the topic/subject phrase ('the mind'). In this poetical context 'mente' refers to the intellectual/principled soul or understanding (not merely 'memory'). The phrase stands alone as the noun phrase picked up by the following relative clause.
segments.paradiso-21-g102: 'che' is a relative pronoun introducing a defining relative clause modifying 'la mente'; 'qui' is an adverb 'here' (i.e. in this heaven/position); 'luce' is used as a present verb meaning 'shines/is radiant' (a poetic form from lucere). The clause literally says 'which shines here.'
segments.paradiso-21-g103: 'in terra' is a simple locative prepositional phrase 'on earth' (i.e. when it was on earth). 'fumma' is an archaic/poetic past form corresponding to fummo ('we were'); here Dante uses it to situate the mind in its earthly condition ('when it was on earth').
segments.paradiso-21-g104: This whole clause gives the consequence and explanation: 'onde' ('hence/therefore') introduces the result; 'riguarda' (3rd sg. present indicative) means 'turns its regard / looks (back) / considers' with the mind as subject; 'come' = 'how'; 'può' = 'can/is able'; 'là giùe' = 'down there' (là + giù, adverbial phrase). 'quel che' = 'that which'; 'non pote' is the past inability ('could not'), and 'perché ’l ciel l’assumma' gives the reason: 'because heaven assumed/received it' (assumma = took up into heaven), with the clitic 'l' referring back to 'quel'. Word order is typical of Dante: reason clause follows ('perché ...').
segments.paradiso-21-g105: 'Sì' here means 'thus' or 'so' and introduces the resulting action; 'mi' is the indirect object pronoun 'to me'; 'prescrisser' is an archaic/past form (past historic) agreeing with the following subject 'le parole sue' — literally 'his words prescribed (to) me' — i.e. 'his words instructed/ordered me.' Word order places the object pronoun before the verb, as is common in older Italian.
segments.paradiso-21-g106: This is a result clause introduced by 'ch’' = 'that': 'io lasciai' (passato remoto, 'I left/abandoned') takes 'la quistione' as direct object. 'e mi ritrassi' is reflexive 'and I withdrew' (ritrassi = passato remoto, 'I drew back/withdrew'); 'a dimandarla' is an infinitive with enclitic object pronoun 'la' ('to ask it' — the question); 'umilmente' is an adverb 'humbly'; finally 'chi fue' ('who he was') is a terse past form (fue = was). The sequence shows first the abandoning of the question, then the speaker's humble action of asking who the person was.
segments.paradiso-21-g107: 'Tra' is the preposition 'between'. '’ due liti d’Italia' names two 'liti' (here: coasts/ridges/shafts — an idiomatic topographical term), with 'd’Italia' specifying the country. 'surgon' is 3rd pl. present 'they rise/are raised' and 'sassi' is the plural subject 'rocks/boulders'. The clause describes a geographic feature: rocks rising between two shores/ridges of Italy.
segments.paradiso-21-g108: 'e' = 'and'; 'non molto distanti' = 'not very distant' (adjective phrase), modifying location; 'a la tua patria' = 'from/near your homeland' (a + definite article + noun). The phrase places the rising rocks geographically 'not far from your native land.'
segments.paradiso-21-g109: 'tanto che' introduces a result clause 'so that'; '’ troni' (the heavenly thrones) is the subject; 'assai' = 'very' / 'much'; 'suonan' (3rd pl. present) = 'they sound/resound'; 'più bassi' = 'lower' (comparative). Together: the presence of these rocks makes the thrones 'sound' or seem 'much lower' — an image of diminished loftiness.
segments.paradiso-21-g110: 'e fanno' = 'and they make/form'; 'un gibbo' is a rounded hump or protuberance; 'che si chiama' is a reflexive construction meaning 'which is called' (si + verb gives an impersonal/passive sense); 'Catria' is the proper name applied to that hump. So the rocks form a hump called Catria.
segments.paradiso-21-g111: 'di sotto al quale' = 'under/beneath which' (relative locative phrase); 'è consecrato' is passive present 'is consecrated/dedicated'; 'un ermo' is the thing consecrated — an 'ermo' is an archaic word for a solitary place or hermitage. The clause states that beneath the hump there is a consecrated hermitage.
segments.paradiso-21-g112: Relative clause: 'che' = 'which'; 'suole' here is the verb solere ('is accustomed/wont') and introduces a customary action (note: the lemma list given is misleading — this 'suole' is a verb, not the noun 'suola'); 'esser disposto' is an infinitive passive/periphrasis 'to be arranged/placed'; 'a sola latria' = 'for sole/only worship' (latria = worship/devotion). The clause means the hermitage is customarily set aside for solitary worship.
segments.paradiso-21-g113: "Così" is an adverb meaning 'thus' and opens the sentence; "ricominciommi" is an archaic, enclitic form of the passato remoto 'ricominciò' + clitic 'mi' (he recommenced to me), so literally 'he began again to me'; "il terzo sermo" is the direct object 'the third speech/discourse'.
segments.paradiso-21-g114: "e poi" is a simple connective meaning 'and then', linking this action to the following one.
segments.paradiso-21-g115: "continüando" is the present participle 'continuing' (note the diaeresis on ü in the manuscript spelling to show vowel separation) and modifies the main verb that follows.
segments.paradiso-21-g116: "disse" is the passato remoto (third person singular) of 'dire' — 'he said' — introducing direct speech.
segments.paradiso-21-g117: "Quivi" is a locative adverb 'there'; "al servigio di Dio" is a prepositional phrase 'to/at the service of God'; "mi" is the clitic object 'me'; "fe'" is an archaic contracted past-tense verb (here functioning as 'made/caused' from 'fare' in the sense 'he made me'); "sì fermo" is the result complement 'so steadfast'. The clitic "mi" appears before the verb, a normal placement in older Italian.
segments.paradiso-21-g118: "che" introduces a result/consequence clause ('that-so that'); "pur" here means 'even' (emphatic); "con cibi di liquor d'ulivi" is a prepositional phrase 'with foods of the liquid of olives' (i.e. simple foods with olive oil), where "liquor" = liquid; "lievemente" (adverb) modifies "passava"; "passava" is the imperfect indicative of 'passare' used to mean 'would endure/undergo' or 'was able to bear'; "caldi e geli" are the things endured — 'heat and cold'.
segments.paradiso-21-g119: "contento" is a predicative adjective 'content/pleased' describing the subject; "ne'" is a poetic contraction of 'in' and introduces the locative phrase "ne' pensier contemplativi" — 'in contemplative thoughts', with "pensier" (shortened form of 'pensieri') followed by the adjective "contemplativi".
segments.paradiso-21-g42: E = and. 'quel che' is a relative phrase 'that which'. 'presso più' is an adverbial phrase meaning 'closest / most near'. 'ci si ritenne' is a reflexive/impersonal construction with ritenne (passato remoto, 3rd singular) meaning 'was held/remained' with ci marking 'to/for us' — together: 'that which remained nearest to us'.
segments.paradiso-21-g43: 'si fé' is the reflexive/passive-like passato remoto of fare (si fece) meaning 'became / made itself'. 'sì chiaro' = 'so clear' (sì = so; chiaro = clear). This result clause ties into the following 'ch’...' introducing the content of what became clear.
segments.paradiso-21-g44: Ch’ = che 'that'. 'io dicea' is the imperfect indicative 'I was saying' or 'I kept saying' (1st sing.), and 'pensando' is a gerund/participial adjunct 'thinking' — together: 'that I kept saying to myself, thinking'.
segments.paradiso-21-g45: Direct speech. 'Io' subject pronoun. 'veggio' is an archaic/poetic form meaning 'I see / I perceive' (here present). 'ben' = 'clearly/well'. 'l’amor' = 'the love'. The relative 'che tu m’accenne' has accenne (passato remoto of accennare) with the clitic m’ = 'to me', so 'that you indicate/kindle in me.'
segments.paradiso-21-g46: Ma = but. 'quella' = 'that (thing)'. 'ond’' (onde/ond) = 'for which / from which' (introducing purpose/relation). 'io aspetto' = present 'I await/expect'. 'il come e ’l quando' = 'the how and the when' (i.e. manner and time). 'del dire e del tacer' = 'of speaking and of silence' — the whole phrase: 'that for which I await how and when to speak or to be silent.'
segments.paradiso-21-g47: 'si sta' is the present of stare with the reflexive/impersonal si and here means 'remains' or 'is held back/abides' — a short predicate saying the matter 'stays' (is left as it is).
segments.paradiso-21-g48: Ond’ io = 'therefore I' or 'so I' (ond = 'therefore/wherefore'), the pronoun io is emphatic: the speaker is drawing the consequence for himself.
segments.paradiso-21-g49: contra ’l disio = 'against the desire' (contra = against; ’l disio = 'the desire') — a prepositional phrase expressing that his action runs counter to his own desire.
segments.paradiso-21-g50: 'fo ben' is an idiomatic older form meaning 'I do well / I act rightly'. The clause 'ch’io non dimando’' = 'that I do not ask' (ch’ = che 'that'; io subject; dimando’ = an archaic form of 'to ask' — here negative). Overall: 'I am right (even) though I do not ask.'
segments.paradiso-21-g51: Per ch’ella = 'for which reason / therefore she'. Per = 'for/because'; ch’ = che 'that/which'; ella = 'she' (subject). It introduces the action she takes because of what preceded.
segments.paradiso-21-g52: Relative clause: 'che vedëa il tacer mio nel veder di colui che tutto vede' = 'who saw my silence in the sight (or seeing) of him who sees everything.' 'vedëa' is imperfect 'she saw'; 'il tacer mio' = 'my silence'; 'nel veder di colui' = 'in the seeing (or sight) of him' (colui = he who); 'che tutto vede' = 'who sees all.'
segments.paradiso-21-g53: 'mi disse' = 'said to me.' 'mi' is the indirect-object clitic 'to me', and 'disse' is the passato remoto of dire (he/she said).
segments.paradiso-21-g54: Direct quoted command. 'Solvi' is an imperative (2nd sing.) from solvo meaning 'loosen/relieve/solve' here used figuratively 'satisfy / relieve'. 'il tuo caldo disio' = 'your hot / burning desire' (caldo = ardent).
segments.paradiso-21-g55: E io incominciai: 'And I began:' — 'E' = and; 'io' emphasised subject; 'incominciai' is passato remoto (I began), introducing direct speech.
segments.paradiso-21-g56: 'La mia mercede non mi fa degno de la tua risposta;' — 'La mia mercede' = 'my merit/reward'. 'non mi fa degno' = 'does not make me worthy' (fa = makes; degno = worthy). 'de la tua risposta' = 'of your answer.' This is a modest refusal: his merit doesn't entitle him to an answer.
segments.paradiso-21-g57: 'ma per colei che ’l chieder mi concede,' — 'but by/for her who grants the asking to me.' 'per colei' = 'by/through her'; '’l chieder' = the act of asking (article + infinitive); 'mi concede' = 'grants me'. It invokes the mediator who allows him to ask.
segments.paradiso-21-g58: 'vita beata che ti stai nascosta dentro a la tua letizia,' — 'blessed life, who/you remain hidden within your own joy.' 'vita beata' is a vocative or address; 'che' introduces the relative/explanatory clause; 'ti stai nascosta' = 'you remain hidden' (stai = you stay); 'dentro a la tua letizia' = 'within your joy.'
segments.paradiso-21-g59: 'fammi nota la cagion che sì presso mi t’ha posta;' — 'fammi nota' is an imperative 'make known to me' (fammi = make to me; nota = known). 'la cagion' = 'the cause/reason.' The relative clause 'che sì presso mi t’ha posta' uses the perfect 't’ha posta' (has placed you) with mi as indirect object: 'that has placed you so near me.'
segments.paradiso-21-g60: 'e dì perché si tace in questa rota la dolce sinfonia di paradiso,' — 'and tell me why the sweet symphony of Paradise is silent in this wheel.' 'dì' imperative 'say/tell'; 'perché' = 'why'; 'si tace' reflexive/passive 'is silent'; 'in questa rota' = 'in this wheel'; the noun phrase following is the subject 'la dolce sinfonia di paradiso.'
segments.paradiso-21-g61: Relative clause modifying 'sinfonia': 'che giù per l’altre suona sì divota' = 'which down through/among the others sounds so devout.' 'giù per l’altre' = 'down among the other (spheres)'; 'suona' present 'sounds'; 'sì divota' = 'so devout / so devoutly' (sì = so; divota = devout).
segments.paradiso-21-g78: Né is a negative conjunction continuing the previous clause (‘nor’/‘not even’). venni is first person singular passato remoto (literally “I came/reached”) and here governs the infinitival complement introduced by a + articolo (‘a l’ultima parola’ = “to the last word” = “reach the last word”). prima is an adverb “before.”
segments.paradiso-21-g79: che is a relative pronoun linking this clause to the preceding ‘ultima parola.’ del suo mezzo = di + il + suo mezzo, a possessive phrase “of his/its midst.” fece is passato remoto of fare meaning “made,” and the unusual word order fece il lume centro means “made the light the center” (object + predicative noun).
segments.paradiso-21-g80: girando is a present participle/gerund (“turning”), modifying the light (it turns while making the center). sé is the reflexive pronoun (here “itself”). come introduces a simile ‘like,’ veloce an adjective ‘swift,’ and mola is a noun (‘mill’/‘millstone’) used in simile; word order is typical poetic inversion.
segments.paradiso-21-g81: poi is an adverb ‘then.’ rispuose is passato remoto of rispondere, meaning ‘answered’ or ‘replied.’ l’amor is a nominalized, personified Love (the article + amor); che v’era dentro is a relative clause: che = ‘that/which,’ v’ (vi) = ‘in it/there,’ era = imperfect ‘was,’ dentro = ‘within.’
segments.paradiso-21-g82: This begins the direct quotation. Luce divina is a noun + adjective (‘divine Light’). sopra me is a prepositional phrase ‘over/upon me.’ s’appunta is the pronominal present indicative of appuntare (si appunta) meaning here “fixes itself / is focussed”; the reflexive s’ (si) shows the action falls on the subject.
segments.paradiso-21-g83: penetrando is a gerund (‘penetrating, piercing’) describing how the Light acts. per questa = ‘through/by means of this,’ and in ch’io m’inventro is a fused relative clause: in + ch’ (in cui/that) + io + m’(mi) + inventro (inventro = ‘I am within/ I find myself inside’), meaning ‘in which I find myself/enclosed.’
segments.paradiso-21-g84: la cui is a relative genitive (‘whose’ = la + cui) referring back to ‘questa.’ virtù is used in the broad medieval sense of power, efficacy, or virtuous power rather than only moral virtue.
segments.paradiso-21-g85: col is the contraction con + il (‘with the’); mio veder is a noun phrase where veder (infinitive used as noun) means ‘my seeing’ or ‘my sight.’ congiunta is a participle/adjective meaning ‘joined’ (agreeing in gender with la virtù understood), so the phrase means ‘whose virtue, joined with my seeing.’
segments.paradiso-21-g86: mi is the indirect/affected object ‘me,’ leva is present indicative of levare (‘raises’/‘lifts’), sopra me = ‘above me,’ and tanto is an adverb ‘so much/so far’ giving the degree of the raising; literal order ‘lifts me above me so much.’
segments.paradiso-21-g87: ch’ is the conjunction ‘that.’ i’ here stands for io (subject pronoun ‘I’) beginning the subordinate clause ch’ i’ veggio = ‘that I see.’ la somma essenza = ‘the highest/supreme essence’; de la quale = ‘of which’ (relative), è = present ‘is,’ munta is a past participle meaning here ‘made/formed/composed from’ (archaic usage).
segments.paradiso-21-g88: Quinci is an adverb ‘from here’ or ‘hence.’ vien is present indicative of venire meaning ‘comes.’ l’allegrezza is the noun ‘the joy/delight.’ ond’ is a contraction of onde/ond (relative/causal ‘whence/with which’), io is subject pronoun, and fiammeggio is first-person present ‘I blaze/flare’ (verb) — Dante often uses figurative verbs of light/ flame.
segments.paradiso-21-g89: per introduces a result/purpose relation here (‘therefore/so’ in older usage). a la vista mia is ‘to/for my sight’ (a + article + noun + possessive), literally ‘as regards my vision.’
segments.paradiso-21-g90: quant’ is a contraction of quanto (‘as much/as far as’) and introduces a concessive/comparative clause; ella = ‘it/she’ refers back to the previously mentioned brightness; è chiara is present indicative ‘is clear/bright.’
segments.paradiso-21-g91: la chiarità = ‘the clarity/brightness.’ de la fiamma = ‘of the flame.’ pareggio is first-person present of pareggiare (here used as an active verb ‘I make equal / I equalize’): Dante often uses pareggio for ‘I make equal / I compare as equal.’
segments.paradiso-21-g92: Ma is the adversative conjunction ‘but.’ quell’ alma = quella + alma, ‘that soul.’ nel ciel = in + il ciel ‘in heaven.’ che più si schiara is a relative clause: che = ‘which’; più si schiara is a reflexive verb phrase meaning ‘becomes more clear/illuminated’ (si schiara = ‘brightens/clears’).
segments.paradiso-21-g93: quel serafin = ‘that seraph.’ che ’n Dio più l’occhio ha fisso is a relative clause: che = ‘who/which’; ’n is contraction of in; Dio = ‘God’; più = ‘more’; l’occhio ha fisso = literal order of ‘has fixed the eye’ = ‘has fixed his gaze’ (ha fisso = perfect with result adjective fisso).
segments.paradiso-21-g94: a la dimanda tua = ‘to/for your question’ (a + article + noun + possessive). non satisfara is future indicative of satisfare (satisfarà): here ‘will not satisfy/fulfil’ the request. Note the negation non placed before the verb.
segments.paradiso-21-g95: però che introduces the reason ‘because.’ sì s’innoltra is a pronominal verb phrase: sì (so/thus) + s’innoltra (si innoltra) = ‘it/that so advances/penetrates onward.’ ne lo abisso = in the abyss (ne = in/of there, lo abisso = the abyss). de l’etterno statuto = ‘of the eternal statute/ordinance’ (de = di). quel che chiedi = ‘that which you ask.’
segments.paradiso-21-g96: che introduces the result clause ‘that’ (continuing the reason). da ogne creata vista = ‘from every created sight’ (da = from, ogne = every, creata = created, vista = sight/vision). è scisso = ‘is cut off / separated’ (present passive/resultative state).
segments.paradiso-21-g97: E is the conjunction ‘and.’ al mondo mortal = a + il + mondo + mortal, literally ‘to the mortal world’ (al = to the). In Dante mondo mortal often means humankind/this earthly life.
segments.paradiso-21-g98: quando tu riedi is a temporal clause ‘when you return’; quando = when, tu = you (subject), riedi is present (or present-subjunctive/poetic present) of riedere/riedire meaning ‘you return.’
segments.paradiso-21-g99: questo rapporta is an imperative-like instruction in narrative: rapporta = present indicative used as a directive ‘report/relate this.’ questo = ‘this’ (the matter just said). The verb rapporta here means ‘tell/report.’
segments.paradiso-21-g100: sì che introduces a purpose/result clause ‘so that’; non presumma is present subjunctive of presumere (‘may not presume/assume’), expressing what should not happen. a tanto segno più mover li piedi literally ‘to such a sign to move the feet further’ = ‘to advance further toward such a sign’; mover li piedi = idiomatic ‘move the feet’ = ‘take another step.’
segments.paradiso-21-g120: 'Render solea' is an archaic periphrasis: the infinitive render + the imperfect solea ('was wont to') means 'used to yield/produce'. Word order is inverted (verb phrase before subject), so 'quel chiostro' is the subject of that verbal periphrasis. The preposition a + 'questi cieli' means 'to/for these heavens', and the adverb 'fertilemente' (postpositive here) modifies the manner of the yielding.
segments.paradiso-21-g121: The conjunction 'e' links this clause to the previous one; 'ora' is the adverb 'now'. 'è fatto' is a compound/resultative construction with auxiliary essere + past participle fatto, meaning 'has been made' / 'has become'; 'vano' describes the resultant state (here 'vain/empty/fruitless').
segments.paradiso-21-g122: 'Sì che' introduces a result clause ('so that'). 'convien' is an impersonal present meaning 'it is necessary' or 'it is fitting'; it takes a subordinate introduced by 'che'. The subordinate 'che si riveli' uses the reflexive/impersonal 'si' and the present subjunctive 'riveli' (subjunctive is required after impersonal expressions of necessity), meaning 'that it be revealed/exposed'. 'Tosto' is an adverb 'soon/quickly' modifying 'convien'.
segments.paradiso-21-g123: 'In quel loco' = 'in that place'. 'fu’' is the past (passato remoto) form 'fu' of essere ('was'); the apostrophe marks an elision typical of the poetic line. The sequence 'fu’ io Pietro Damiano' places the subject after the verb for emphasis: 'I was Peter Damian.'
segments.paradiso-21-g124: The conjunction 'e' links the identification of a second person. 'Pietro Peccator fu’' again uses passato remoto 'fu’' = 'was'. 'Ne la casa' is an archaic alternative to modern 'nella casa' ('in the house'); 'di Nostra Donna' = 'of Our Lady' (apposition/title). 'In sul lito adriano' combines 'in' + 'sul' (on the) to mean 'on the Adriatic shore' — a fixed locative phrase.
segments.paradiso-21-g125: "Poca vita mortal" (little mortal life) places the adjective before the noun for emphasis; "m'era rimasa" uses the clitic "m'" (to me) + the auxiliary 'era' with the past participle 'rimasa' (archaic spelling of 'rimasta') — literally 'was left to me'. The participle agrees with 'vita' (feminine).
segments.paradiso-21-g126: "quando" = 'when'; "fui" is passato remoto of 'essere' used with past participles to form a passive meaning 'I was'; "chiesto" and "tratto" are past participles functioning in that passive ('asked/summoned' and 'drawn/taken'); "a quel cappello" = 'to that cap/hat' (literal object of being brought).
segments.paradiso-21-g127: "che" introduces a relative clause modifying 'quel cappello'; "pur" again means 'still/even'; the idiom "di male in peggio" literally 'from bad into worse' (i.e. from bad to worse); "si travasa" is a pronominal verb form meaning 'it pours/transfers itself' or 'it runs over' — a reflexive/passive sense conveying that the thing moves into a worse state.
segments.paradiso-21-g128: "Venne" is the passato remoto 'came' used twice for rhetorical repetition; "Cefàs" is Cephas/Peter; "il gran vasello de lo Spirito Santo" is a noun phrase 'the great vessel of the Holy Spirit' ("de" = 'of' in older spelling). The doubled verb gives parallel arrivals.
segments.paradiso-21-g129: "magri e scalzi" are predicative adjectives ('thin/lean and barefoot') agreeing in gender/number with the compound subject; the short, balanced phrase describes their outward condition.
segments.paradiso-21-g130: "prendendo" is the present participle 'taking' and links behaviorally to the subjects ('they, while coming, taking ...'); "il cibo da qualunque ostello" is a direct object and prepositional phrase 'food from any inn', with "qualunque" meaning 'any/whichever' (generalizing the source).

sentences.paradiso-21-s5: If anyone knew what nourishment my face took in that blessed expression when I turned my attention to another concern, he would know how pleasing it was to me to obey my heavenly guide, weighing one side against the other.
sentences.paradiso-21-s6: Inside the crystal that bears the word encircling the world of its dear leader — beneath whom every malice lay dead — of a golden color in which a ray shines through, I saw a stair erected so far upward that my light could not follow it.
sentences.paradiso-21-s7: I also saw down the steps so many radiances descend that I thought every light that appears in heaven was diffused from there.
sentences.paradiso-21-s8: And as, by the natural custom, the poles together at daybreak move to warm their cold feathers; then some go away without return, others turn themselves toward the place from which they were moved, and others, wheeling, make a pause; in such a way it seemed to me that there was there, in that flashing which came together, just as it struck in a certain degree.
sentences.paradiso-21-s33: They came and halted around this one, and let out a cry of so high a sound that it could not be matched here; nor did I hear it, so the thunder overwhelmed me.
sentences.paradiso-21-s1: Already my eyes were fixed again upon the face of my lady, and my spirit with them, and had withdrawn from every other purpose.
sentences.paradiso-21-s2: And she did not laugh; but, 'If I were to laugh,' she began to me, 'you would become like Semele when she was turned to ashes: for my beauty, which grows more luminous as one climbs the stairway of the eternal palace, as you have seen, the higher one ascends, if it were not tempered would shine so much that your mortal power, before its brightness, would be a leafy bough that topples a throne.'
sentences.paradiso-21-s3: We had risen to the seventh splendour, which now sends mixed rays downward from beneath the breast of the ardent Lion of its power.
sentences.paradiso-21-s4: Fix your mind behind your eyes, and make of those mirrors a likeness that in this mirror will appear to you.
sentences.paradiso-21-s13: You have mortal hearing just as you have sight, he answered me; therefore here one does not sing because Beatrice has not smiled.
sentences.paradiso-21-s14: Down the steps of the holy ladder I descended only so far to do you honor, with words and with the light that mantles me; nor did greater love make me more ready, for greater and such love seethes up from here, as the flaming shows you.
sentences.paradiso-21-s15: But the lofty charity that makes us ready servants to the counsel which governs the world distributes here by lot, as you observe.
sentences.paradiso-21-s16: I see well, I said, 'sacred lamp: how free love in this court suffices to follow eternal providence; but this is the thing that seems hard for me to discern, because you alone were predestined to this office among your companions.'
sentences.paradiso-21-s29: Now they want, from here and from there, someone to shore up the modern pastors and someone to lead them.
sentences.paradiso-21-s30: Their palfreys cover them with their mantles, so that two beasts go under one skin: O patience, how much you endure!
sentences.paradiso-21-s32: At that voice I saw more little flames descend and turn, rank upon rank, and with every circle they made them more beautiful.
sentences.paradiso-21-s21: The mind that shines here was on earth; hence it looks to see how, down there, that which could not (be done) — because heaven took it up.
sentences.paradiso-21-s22: Thus his words instructed me, so I left the question and withdrew to ask it humbly who he was.
sentences.paradiso-21-s23: Between the two banks/shores of Italy stones rise, and not far from your native land, so that the thrones sound much lower, and they form a hump called Catria, beneath which a hermitage is consecrated, which is wont to be arranged for solitary worship alone.
sentences.paradiso-21-s24: Thus the third discourse began again to me; and then, continuing, he said: “There, in the service of God, he made me so steadfast that, even on simple foods of olive oil I could lightly endure heat and cold, content in contemplative thoughts.”
sentences.paradiso-21-s9: And that which remained nearest to us became so clear that I kept saying to myself: 'I clearly perceive the love that you kindle in me.'
sentences.paradiso-21-s10: But that — for which I wait to know the how and the when of speaking and of keeping silent — stays; therefore I, against my desire, do well in not asking.
sentences.paradiso-21-s11: Therefore she, who saw my silence in the sight of Him who sees all, said to me: 'Satisfy your burning desire.'
sentences.paradiso-21-s12: And I began: 'My merit does not make me worthy of your answer; but for her who grants me the asking — blessed life that you remain hidden within your own joy — make known to me the cause that has placed you so near me; and tell me why the sweet symphony of Paradise is silent in this wheel, which down below among the others sounds so devout.'
sentences.paradiso-21-s17: Nor had I reached the final word when the light of his midst made the light the center, turning itself like a swift mill; then answered the Love that was within it: "Divine Light fixes itself above me, penetrating through this in which I am enclosed; whose power, joined with my seeing, lifts me above myself so much that I see the supreme essence from which it is formed."
sentences.paradiso-21-s18: From this comes the joy with which I blaze; therefore, to my sight — insofar as it is clear — I make the clarity of the flame equal.
sentences.paradiso-21-s19: But that soul in heaven which becomes clearest, that seraph who has fixed his gaze more upon God, will not satisfy your question, because what you ask so advances into the abyss of the eternal ordinance that it is cut off from every created sight.
sentences.paradiso-21-s20: And report this to the mortal world when you return, so that it may not presume to move its feet any further toward so great a sign.
sentences.paradiso-21-s25: That cloister used to yield abundant fruit to these heavens; but now it has become vain, so that it must soon be revealed.
sentences.paradiso-21-s26: In that place I was Peter Damian, and Peter Peccator was in the house of Our Lady on the Adriatic shore.
sentences.paradiso-21-s27: Little mortal life was left to me, when I was summoned and brought to that cap, which still pours itself from bad into worse.
sentences.paradiso-21-s28: Cephas came and the great vessel of the Holy Spirit came, thin and barefoot, taking food from any inn.
